'''''Star Wars: Super Bombad Racing''''' is a 2001 PlayStation 2 kart racer released shortly after [[Star Wars: Starfighter (video game)|''Star Wars: Starfighter'']]. Using characters and locations from [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']], the game challenges you to race up to eight CPU opponents or four of your friends, using weapons and gadgets to gain the upper hand. A secondary arena battle mode allowed pure combat between racers.

The game's trademark was the appearance of the characters: in similar style to ''Mario Kart'' for the Nintendo 64, the characters all had oversized heads and piloted shrunken vehicles. The vehicles, however, all hovered, making ''Super Bombad Racing'' a unique skill to master in the handling department.

Developed by [[Lucas Learning]] and published by [[LucasArts]], the game was mainly aimed at children thanks to the "cute" cartoon appearance of the characters and locations. Several of the characters have been toned down from their appearances in [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']], and character taunts between racers are all kid-friendly. Each racer has a special, racer-exclusive weapon or ability. Musically, the game features recognizable themes from the [[John Williams]] film scores, but all have been reworked into childish, pop-type beats.

==Gameplay==
[[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] and an [[Armored Assault Tank/Legends|AAT]] are hidden cheat characters, and as such have no specific special weapons or attributes. Several cheats are available that turn all the text on the menu screens into distinctive [[Jawa/Legends|Jawa]] phrases or the Battle Droid's ''"Roger roger!"'' callsign. Yet another cheat can turn all the racers, no matter which character you've chosen, into [[Kaadu/Legends|kaadu]] on treadmills.

===Racers===
*[[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]] in Sith Infiltrator (special weapon: double-bladed lightsaber)
*[[Jar Jar Binks/Legends|Jar Jar Binks]] in [[Tribubble bongo/Legends|tribubble bongo]] (special weapon: elasticated tongue)
*[[Sebulba/Legends|Sebulba]] in Podracer (special weapon: homing junk toss)
*[[Rugor Nass/Legends|Boss Nass]] in [[Gungan Manta-Ray]] (special weapon: blinding spit-spray)
*[[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] in [[Radiant VII/Legends|''Radiant VII'']] (special weapon: Force Levitate)
*[[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] in Jedi Council Chair (special weapon: Force Invert)
*[[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] in [[N-1 starfighter/Legends|N-1 starfighter]] (special weapon: energy coupler)
*[[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Queen Amidala]] in [[J-type 327 Nubian royal starship/Legends|Naboo Royal Starship]] (special weapon: blinding light blast)

===Courses===
*'''Circuit One:'''
**Naboo Swamp
**Dune Sea
**Theed City
*'''Circuit Two:'''
**Podrace
**Otoh Gunga
**Mos Espa
*'''Circuit Three:'''
**Coruscant
**Power Station
**Droid Control Ship

===Arenas===
*Naboo Battlefield
*Jedi Temple
*Great Pit of Carkoon
*Hoth Asteroid

===Unlockable Content===
*[[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] in TIE Advanced x1 (special weapon: Death Star attack)
*Space Freighter arena
*[[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] in Slave 1
*[[Armored Assault Tank/Legends|AAT]]

==Development==
{{Quote|Our goal was to make a game that a kid could pick up and just play, enjoying the spirit and the art style.|Project Leader [[Michael McCormick]]|<ref name="Gamer2">{{GamerCite|2|State of the Arts}}</ref>}}
There are several variations on the box art: Darth Maul alone, Darth Maul with Yoda, Darth Maul with Yoda and Jar Jar Binks, and Darth Maul with Yoda and Sebulba.

==Continuity==
Rather than fitting within the continuity of the [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]], the game was "made purely for fun" according to [[Leland Chee]],<ref name="Holocron continuity">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=02178#13682994|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|author=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2005-12-12|time=12:17 PM|quote=The short answer is, the stuff that fits. There has definitely been a greater effort to make newer games fit within the rest EU than in years past. Then again, some games are made purely for fun such as games like Demolition, Master of Teras Kasi, Yoda Stories, and Super Bombad Racing.}}</ref> who maintains [[Holocron continuity database]].<ref name="LinkedIn">{{LinkedIn|url=in/holocron|text=Leland Chee|archiveurl=https://archive.is/byVHT}}</ref>
